source: Carlos Jumbo guitar plans archive (3-page set: JUMBO-CARLOS-1-3.pdf, JUMBO-CARLOS-2-3.pdf, JUMBO-CARLOS-3-3.pdf). Vectorizer Phase 3.6 analysis across all 3 pages â€” page 3 yielded validated body outline (422x498mm, 0.946x scale). Body catalog reference: 400x520mm (39-point DXF, AI vision extraction). Spanish-style jumbo acoustic with wide body for rich bass response.

standard.description: Carlos Jumbo standard â€” a Spanish-influenced jumbo-sized acoustic guitar. Wide lower bout and deep body produce a commanding bass-heavy tone with full projection. Traditional fan bracing or X-bracing depending on builder preference.

neck.note: Wide classical-style nut width (52mm) for fingerstyle playing. Flat fretboard traditional for Spanish instruments. Body join at 12th fret â€” standard classical/jumbo junction. Cedar neck common for Spanish-influenced designs.

headstock.note: Traditional slotted headstock with open-gear tuners. Spanish jumbo style.

steel_string.description: Carlos Jumbo steel-string variant â€” adapted for steel strings with reinforced bracing and narrower nut. Pin bridge replaces classical tie bridge.

neck.note: Narrower nut width for steel-string playing. Body join at 14th fret for better upper fret access. Slight neck angle for steel-string bridge height.

headstock.note: Solid headstock with enclosed tuners for the steel-string variant.

body.outline_source: acoustic/carlos_jumbo_body.dxf (39-point AI vision extraction from PDF plans)

outline_dxf_dimensions.note: Body catalog lists 400x520mm from AI vision extraction. Vectorizer Phase 3.6 page 3 detected 422x498mm at 0.946x scale (scaled via single_coil_route reference 89.9mm to 85.0mm). The scale reference may not be fully applicable to acoustic plans. Catalog dimensions are considered more reliable for this model.

body.note: The Carlos Jumbo is a wide-body Spanish-influenced flat-top acoustic guitar (not an archtop). Its lower bout is wider than a standard dreadnought (430mm vs 400mm), producing a louder, bass-forward tone with excellent projection. The rounded jumbo shape with gentle waist curve is characteristic of Spanish-school design. Vectorizer page 3 detected 2 shapes classified as f-holes â€” these are misclassifications (likely rosette segments or bracing outlines), confirmed by research that the Carlos Jumbo is a round-soundhole flat-top design.

calibration.note: Dimensions derived from 1:1 PDF blueprint using soundhole (102mm) as calibration reference. Volume validated against jumbo acoustic range (14-19 liters). Previous dimensions (430/310/270/520) were estimated; these are measured from calibrated blueprint.

soundhole.note: Standard 4-inch (102mm) soundhole with classical-style mosaic rosette. Center positioned relative to top of body at the 12th-fret join area.

classical_variant.note: Traditional 7-fan brace pattern for classical/nylon string variant. Fans radiate from below the soundhole toward the lower bout.

steel_string_variant.note: Scalloped X-brace for steel-string variant. Standard acoustic steel-string reinforcement.

back_bracing.note: 4 lateral back braces. Standard acoustic construction.

vectorizer_bracing_detections.note: Vectorizer detected 35 bracing features across all 3 plan pages. Page 1 had the most bracing detail (20 features), likely the primary construction view. Page 2 shows section views (9 bracing). Page 3 shows body outline with some bracing (6).

classical_variant.note: Classical tie bridge for nylon strings. No pin holes â€” strings are tied through the bridge.

steel_string_variant.note: Pin bridge for steel strings. Compensated bone saddle for accurate intonation.

side_profile.note: Deeper than a dreadnought (J-45: 124mm tail, 98mm neck). The extra depth contributes to the jumbo's bass volume.

neck_joint.note: Traditional dovetail neck joint at the 12th fret (classical variant) or 14th fret (steel-string variant). Dovetail provides strong, resonant joint for acoustic projection.

cnc_operations.note: Acoustic guitar CNC operations differ significantly from solid-body electrics. Operations cover the top plate, back plate, side bending form, and neck â€” not a single body blank. Listed operations are for a CNC-assisted lutherie workflow.

T1.name: 6mm Flat End Mill
T1.use: Soundhole, bridge plate recess, rosette channel, top/back perimeter

T2.name: 3mm Flat End Mill
T2.use: Binding ledge, bracing slots, detail routing

T4.name: 1.5mm Drill
T4.use: Bridge pin holes, tuner holes, pilot holes

top_plate_operations.name: Top Plate Perimeter
top_plate_operations.note: Cut top plate to body outline shape, slightly oversized for binding ledge

top_plate_operations.name: Soundhole Cut
top_plate_operations.note: 102mm diameter soundhole through the top plate

top_plate_operations.name: Rosette Channel
top_plate_operations.note: Concentric channel for mosaic rosette inlay

top_plate_operations.name: Binding Ledge Top
top_plate_operations.note: Stepped ledge around top perimeter for binding

top_plate_operations.name: Bridge Plate Recess
top_plate_operations.note: Shallow recess for bridge plate reinforcement under soundhole

top_plate_operations.name: Top Radius Dishing
top_plate_operations.note: Optional: CNC dish for top plate radius (7.6m / 25-foot radius)

back_plate_operations.name: Back Plate Perimeter
back_plate_operations.note: Cut back plate to body outline, slightly oversized

back_plate_operations.name: Back Radius Dishing
back_plate_operations.note: Optional: CNC dish for back plate radius (4.6m / 15-foot radius)

bending_form_operations.name: Side Bending Form Male
bending_form_operations.note: Male mold for side bending â€” CNC from MDF or plywood

bending_form_operations.name: Side Bending Form Female
bending_form_operations.note: Female mold, offset by side thickness (2mm) from male form

neck_operations.name: Neck Blank Profile
neck_operations.note: Cut neck blank to heel/shaft profile outline

neck_operations.name: Headstock Angle Cut
neck_operations.note: 14-degree headstock scarf joint or carved angle

neck_operations.name: Truss Rod Channel
neck_operations.note: 6mm x 10mm truss rod channel in neck shaft

neck_operations.name: Heel Dovetail Tenon
neck_operations.note: Precision dovetail tenon on neck heel

neck_operations.name: Fret Slot Cutting
neck_operations.note: 20 precision fret slots â€” CNC ensures accurate spacing

bridge_pin_holes.name: Bridge Pin Holes 6x
bridge_pin_holes.note: Steel-string variant only â€” 6 bridge pin holes through top plate and bridge plate

top_plate.note: Bookmatched top plate blank. Slightly oversized for binding overhang and perimeter cleanup.

back_plate.note: Bookmatched back plate. Same oversizing as top.

sides.note: Two side pieces, bent to shape using bending form.

neck_blank.note: Oversized neck blank for carving. Include extra for headstock angle or scarf joint.

page_1.note: Full construction plan page. Oversize detection (821x574mm) â€” multi-view layout page with neck pocket reference that does not match acoustic body scale. Bracing detail strongest on this page (20 detected).

page_2.note: Section views and detail page. Multiple neck pocket detections suggest cross-section drawings. Control cavity detections (27) likely correspond to bracing cross-section outlines being misclassified on an acoustic plan.

page_3.note: Body outline page â€” only page to pass validation. 422x498mm (close to catalog 400x520mm). 2 f-hole detections are misclassifications â€” the Carlos Jumbo is a flat-top with a round soundhole, not an archtop. Likely rosette segments or bracing outlines triggered the f-hole classifier. Scale reference from single_coil_route may not be optimal for acoustic plans.

dxf_assets.note: Primary body outline from acoustic/carlos_jumbo_body.dxf (39-point AI vision extraction, pre-existing). Vectorizer Phase 3.6 outputs from all 3 plan pages provide additional construction detail. Page 3 validated at 422x498mm. The 3-page plan set contains body outline, bracing patterns, section views, and construction details.

design_heritage.note: The Carlos Jumbo draws from the Spanish guitar-making tradition with its wide body, deep projection, and classical construction options. The jumbo body size provides exceptional bass response and volume â€” the largest standard acoustic body shape. This is a flat-top acoustic with a round soundhole (not an archtop). Plans show both classical (nylon) and steel-string construction details.

notes: The Carlos Jumbo is a Spanish-influenced jumbo acoustic guitar with a wide body for rich bass and projection.
Three-page plan set: page 1 (construction details, bracing layout), page 2 (section views, neck details), page 3 (body outline).
Vectorizer processed all 3 pages â€” 2,011 total primitives across 124 seconds of processing time.
Only page 3 passed body validation (422x498mm). Pages 1-2 contain multi-view layouts that exceed single-body size detection.
Existing body outline in catalog: acoustic/carlos_jumbo_body.dxf (39-point extraction, 400x520mm).
Vectorizer detected 35 bracing features total across all pages â€” strong acoustic construction detail in the plans.

Page 3 detected 2 shapes classified as f-holes â€” confirmed as vectorizer misclassifications. The Carlos Jumbo is a flat-top acoustic with a round soundhole, not an archtop. Likely rosette segments or bracing outlines triggered the f-hole classifier.
CNC operations for acoustic guitars differ fundamentally from solid-body electrics: separate top plate, back plate, side bending forms, and neck operations rather than a single body blank.
Scale length is 650mm (25.59 inches) â€” standard for classical and jumbo Spanish-style guitars.
Two variants defined: classical (52mm nut, flat fretboard, tie bridge, 12th-fret join) and steel-string (44.5mm nut, radiused fretboard, pin bridge, 14th-fret join).
